text: In Agile methodology, work is broken into small, manageable cycles called sprints. Each sprint usually lasts two to four weeks and focuses on delivering a specific set of features. The team starts with a backlog, which is a prioritized list of tasks or user stories. A user story describes a small function from the end user's perspective. Every day, the team has a short standup meeting to discuss progress, plans, and any problems. Because Agile uses iterative development, the product improves gradually through repeated cycles, allowing for adjustments based on feedback. This approach helps teams stay flexible and deliver value quickly.
